plugin.manualsprite.block.body: Stock Paper2D in Unreal is fine for simple sprites but gives no precise control over geometry. ManualSprite adds a new `Manual Sprite` asset with its own editor: Pixel-perfect collisions — place vertices exactly where you need them. Optimized complexity — no extra triangles. 3D mesh generation (`StaticMesh` / `SkeletalMesh`) straight from 2D — with materials and a basic skeleton.
plugin.manualsprite.card.body: Dedicated window with viewport, Details panel, and a multi-function toolbar — not embedded in the Paper2D editor.
plugin.manualsprite.card.title: Dedicated asset editor
plugin.manualsprite.description: Manual 2D geometry editor for PaperSprite — build pixel-perfect collisions and generate StaticMesh / SkeletalMesh straight from 2D art.
plugin.manualsprite.getting-started.bloc: Stack mirrors: XY enabled = one edit copies into four quadrants. Fastest way to build a front-facing character.
plugin.manualsprite.getting-started.desc: Step-by-step guide for Manual Sprite Editor Tools — from install to mesh generation.
plugin.manualsprite.getting-started.h.ша: Step 1 — Create a Manual Sprite asset
plugin.manualsprite.getting-started.imag: Toolbar: History — Modes — Mirror — Grid — Tools — Generate
plugin.manualsprite.getting-started.step: Open the plugin on FAB Marketplace and hit Install to Engine — it auto-downloads into your UE version.
plugin.manualsprite.getting-started.авто: `V` — intersection check. Red edges = crossing each other, needs a rebuild.
`4` — delete all triangles tied to selected vertices (quick cleanup).
plugin.manualsprite.getting-started.зерк: Toggled via X, Y, XY buttons on the toolbar. Moving a vertex moves its mirror symmetrically — handy for symmetric characters and objects.
plugin.manualsprite.getting-started.шаг-: In the Content Browser right-click → Paper2D → Manual Sprite. Name the asset.
Double-click the `Manual Sprite` asset. In the Details panel on the right:

| Mode | Key | Action | |-------|---------|----------| | Select | `Q` | Move vertices (click or marquee) | | Add Vertex | `W` | Click in viewport — add a new vertex | | Triangle | `E` | Select 3 vertices — form a triangle | | Delete | `R` | Click a vertex or triangle — delete |
Press `Ctrl+M` or hit Generate on the toolbar. The Mesh Generation dialog opens:

| Setting | Description | |-----------|----------| | Mesh Type | `StaticMesh` or `SkeletalMesh` | | Pivot Placement | `Center` / `Bottom` / `Origin` / `Custom` — object pivot point | | Asset Name & Path | Name and path for new assets | | Material Settings | Auto-create Lit/Unlit material, two-sided |
Hit Generate — new assets appear in the Content Browser and get highlighted.
plugin.manualsprite.image.alt: ManualSprite Editor toolbar with modes and settings
plugin.manualsprite.image.caption: Toolbar: History · Modes · Mirror · Grid · Tools · Generate
plugin.manualsprite.reference.block.body: The plugin uses APIs added in UE 5.5. Running on 5.0–5.4 is not guaranteed. Need older-version support — ping the author.
plugin.manualsprite.reference.descriptio: Toolbar, hotkeys, Project Settings, and technical details for ManualSprite Editor Tools.
plugin.manualsprite.reference.project-se: Edit → Project Settings → Plugins → Manual Sprite Mesh Generator
Defaults for the mesh generation dialog live here: mesh type, pivot placement, material params, path for new assets.
plugin.manualsprite.reference.title: Reference
plugin.manualsprite.reference.video.titl: Manual Sprite Editor — geometry editing and mesh generation
plugin.manualsprite.reference.горячие-кл: | Key | Action | |---------|----------| | `Q` | Select mode | | `W` | Add Vertex mode | | `E` | Triangle mode | | `R` | Delete mode | | `X` | Mirror on X | | `Y` | Mirror on Y | | `Shift+X` | Mirror on XY | | `G` | Toggle grid | | `Ctrl+G` | Toggle grid snap | | `3` | Delaunay auto-triangulation (selected vertices) | | `4` | Delete all triangles on selected vertices | | `V` | Check triangulation for intersections | | `Ctrl+Z` / `Ctrl+Y` | Undo / Redo | | `Ctrl+C` / `Ctrl+V` | Copy / Paste vertices | | `Ctrl+M` | Open mesh generation dialog | | `Delete` | Delete selected vertices/triangles | | Mouse wheel | Zoom | | Middle button | Pan view |
plugin.manualsprite.reference.тулбар.p1: | Group | Buttons | Description | |--------|--------|----------| | History | Undo, Redo | Undo / redo actions | | Modes | Select, Add, Triangle, Delete | Switch editing modes | | Mirror | X, Y, XY | Mirror editing on axes | | Grid | Grid, Snap, Size | Grid visibility, snap, size | | Tools | (dropdown) | Copy, select, utilities | | Generation | Generate | Mesh generation dialog |
plugin.manualsprite.tagline: Fills the gap in stock Paper2D: place vertices by hand, assemble triangles, generate StaticMesh and SkeletalMesh directly from 2D art — with materials, skeleton, and pivot control.
plugin.manualsprite.title: ManualSprite Editor Tools
plugin.manualsprite.video.title: Manual Sprite Editor — full walkthrough
plugin.manualsprite.что-дальше.p1: How to use — step by step from asset creation to mesh export.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.block.body: The plugin captures all mouse events globally — regardless of what's active on screen (UMG, level, editor). Events arrive via delegates, hooked up in Blueprint in a few nodes.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.block.title: What it is
plugin.mouseinterceptor.card.body: Captures mouse events regardless of active UI or input state.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.card.title: Global interception
plugin.mouseinterceptor.description: Global mouse event interception for Unreal Engine — no ticks, Blueprint delegates, configurable double-click.
The plugin always detects double-click. To effectively disable detection — set the threshold very high: `SetDoubleClickThreshold(10.0f)`. Then `bIsDoubleClick` will almost always be `false`.
Add MouseInterceptorComponent, subscribe to mouse delegates in Blueprint, tune the double-click threshold.
Open the plugin on FAB Marketplace and hit Install to Engine — it auto-downloads into your UE version.
Default threshold is `0.25` seconds. Change it at runtime via `SetDoubleClickThreshold`.
In the actor's Blueprint Event Graph:
`bIsDoubleClick` — `true` if the click fell inside the `DoubleClickThreshold` window from the previous click of the same button.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.image.caption: Component in the Components panel
The component registers its own `IInputProcessor` in `FSlateApplication` on `BeginPlay` and unregisters it on `EndPlay`. No Tick, no polling.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.descri: API, delegates, and FAQ for Mouse Interceptor.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.getdou: Returns the current threshold in seconds.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.h.рабо: Does interception work while a UI widget is active?
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.h.что-: What about right-click and the UE context menu?
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.onmous: | Parameter | Type | Description | |----------|-----|----------| | `Button` | `FKey` | Pressed button (`EKeys::LeftMouseButton`, `RightMouseButton`, `MiddleMouseButton`, `ThumbMouseButton`, `ThumbMouseButton2`) | | `bIsDoubleClick` | `bool` | `true` if click fell inside the `DoubleClickThreshold` window from the previous click of the same button |
| Parameter | Type | Description | |----------|-----|----------| | `Button` | `FKey` | Released button | | `bIsDoubleClick` | `bool` | Double-click flag for the press+release pair |
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.setdou: Sets the interval (seconds) used to detect a double-click.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.title: Reference
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.как-от: Set `DoubleClickThreshold` to a very large value — e.g. `10.0f`. The `bIsDoubleClick` flag will be `false` almost always.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.компон: `UMouseInterceptorComponent` — a `UActorComponent`, attach it to any actor via the Components panel.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.поддер: No — the plugin handles input on the client side only. For networked clicks, wrap delegates in RPCs manually.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.работа: Yes — interception is global, independent of input focus. Works when the cursor is outside the window? No — Slate only gets events while the window is active.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.совмес: Yes — the plugin runs alongside Enhanced Input (both listen to Slate). Two separate input layers, no conflict.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.reference.что-с-: The plugin receives events before Slate's default handling. If you need to keep default behavior (e.g. RMB in a UMG widget), handle the `bIsHandled` flag in your delegate — return `false` to pass the event through.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.tagline: Lightweight Unreal Engine plugin for global mouse interception. OnMousePressed / OnMouseReleased delegates, configurable double-click threshold, no ticks.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.title: Mouse Interceptor
plugin.mouseinterceptor.поддерживаемые-в: The plugin ships as CrossEngine — the same source compiles on UE 4.27 and every UE 5 line up to 5.7.
plugin.mouseinterceptor.что-дальше.p1: How to use — step by step: add the component, subscribe to delegates, set the threshold.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.card.body: UE 4.27 and 5.0–5.7 from a single source. Migrate projects between versions without rebuilding the pipeline.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.card.title: One build — 9 engines
plugin.spriteOptimizer.description: Professional sprite packing & optimization plugin for Unreal Engine 4.27–5.7.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.bo: Atlas Mode is the main mode. Packs multiple textures into a single atlas, generates sprites and Material Instances with pixel-based UV params.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.de: Pack textures into atlas sheets with an interactive editor.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.dr: Drag textures from the Content Browser straight into the Items panel — they appear as Unplaced, then hit Place All or Analyze.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.ma: | Parameter | Description | |----------|----------| | PixelX, PixelY | Sprite position in the atlas (pixels) | | SizeW, SizeH | Sprite size (pixels) | | AtlasW, AtlasH | Atlas size (pixels) | | UVRotation | Rotation flag (for rotated packs) |
Works in 3D meshes, Paper2D sprites, and UMG widgets.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.ал: | Algorithm | Best for | |----------|----------------| | Max Rects | Universal. 5 heuristics × 4 sort orders. Industry standard. | | Bin Pack | Guillotine/support-point. Good for mixed sizes. | | Fixed Grid | Uniform N×M grid. Best pick for tile-based games. | | Tile (Simple) | Horizontal/vertical strip. For linear layouts. |
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.вы: After Create Atlas (`Ctrl+Shift+Enter`), the Content Browser shows:
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.ко: Overlapping items are highlighted red, the status bar shows a "Collisions!" warning. Enable Free Move (`F`) to allow overlaps.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.му: If items don't fit on a single page, the plugin auto-creates extra pages. Switch via the tab bar above the viewport.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.на: | Option | Description | |-------|----------| | Texture | Always created — BC7 compression with alpha | | Sprite | PaperSprite assets | | Material Instance | MI with pixel UV params | | Preserve Position | Adjust pivot after trim | | Pad to x4 | Sizes padded to multiples of 4 for BC compression |
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.от: Content Browser → select 2+ textures → RMB → Sprite Optimizer → Atlas.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.ст: | Panel | Description | |--------|----------| | Items (left) | List of items: Placed and Unplaced. Search, filter, checkboxes. | | Viewport (center) | Interactive canvas — move, rotate, select. | | Settings (right) | Algorithm, page size, output params. |
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.ту: | Button | Shortcut | Description | |--------|---------|----------| | Analyze | `F5` | Run the packing algorithm and show preview | | Create Atlas | `Ctrl+Shift+Enter` | Export atlas, sprites, Material Instances | | Repack | `Ctrl+R` | Repack the current page | | Place All | — | Place all Unplaced items | | Import Atlas | — | Import an existing atlas | | Fit | `Home` | Fit view to the whole atlas | | Grid | `G` | Toggle grid | | Snap | `S` | Toggle grid snap | | Free Move | `F` | Free movement (no collisions) |
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.atlas.уп: | Action | How | |----------|-----| | Select item | LMB on item | | Add to selection | Ctrl+LMB | | Rubber band | LMB drag on empty area | | Move | LMB drag on item | | Axis lock | Shift+drag — locks to H or V (auto-detect) | | Rotate 90° | `R` on selected | | Remove from atlas | `Del` — moves to Unplaced |
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.import-a: Auto-detects regions by transparency (flood fill). Yellow outlines = detected islands.
Import Atlas splits an existing atlas texture back into individual items that can be re-edited in the Atlas Editor.
Reverse-engineer an existing atlas back into editable elements.
Slices the texture with a uniform grid.
Cell Width / Cell Height — cell size
If the atlas already has linked Sprites or Material Instances, a source-picker dialog appears:
Yes (Sprites) — restore regions from sprite data
No (Material Instances) — restore from MI UV params
If there's no metadata — Slice Texture opens automatically.
Content Browser → RMB on atlas texture → Sprite Optimizer → Import Atlas.
Hit Slice — textures are saved in a `Sliced/` subfolder, and a fresh Atlas Editor opens with those items.

| Action | How | |----------|-----| | Select region | LMB | | Draw new | LMB drag on empty area | | Delete | Select → `Del` | | Resize | Drag edges/corners of selected | | Mark as rotated | Select → `R` (cleared on export) | | Zoom / Pan | Mouse wheel / RMB or MMB |
Green = selected region. Orange with "R" = marked as rotated.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.optimize: Optimize Mode trims transparent pixels from texture edges, shrinking size significantly — while sprites stay visually in place in the scene.
Trim transparent edges while preserving sprite pivot.
How to open
Settings
Workflow
Tips
Pivot preservation (Preserve Position)
Optimize Mode
Content Browser → select textures → RMB → Sprite Optimizer → Optimize.

| Parameter | Description | |----------|----------| | Alpha Threshold | Pixels with alpha ≤ threshold are treated as transparent and trimmed | | Preserve Position | Adjust pivot so the sprite doesn't shift visually | | Pad to x4 | Pad to a multiple of 4 — required for BC compression | | Output subfolder | Where trimmed textures go (set in Project Settings) |
The editor opens with the selected textures listed.
Hit F5 (Analyze) — the plugin computes trimmed sizes and memory savings.
Review results: yellow = good savings, orange = size grew (padding expanded area).
Optionally tweak Alpha Threshold — the transparency cutoff.
Hit Ctrl+Shift+Enter — trimmed textures are saved.
Alpha Threshold defaults to 0 (fully transparent pixels). Bump to 1–5 to trim the semi-transparent "halo" around objects.
If trim grows size (orange) — the texture has barely any transparent edges, trimming isn't worth it.
Trim runs on the original without mutation — the source file is never touched, a new one is created.
Preserve Pos ✓ is the key feature. After trim the plugin auto-adjusts the sprite's pivot so it stays at the same visual spot in the scene.
Real example from the README:
6 textures 2048×2048 trimmed to actual content. 32.2 MB → 186 KB (99% saved). Scene didn't break — everything stays where it was.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.features.project-: Sprite Optimizer Project — a new asset type (landed in v2.1). Stores the full state of an editing session: layout, page sizes, per-item trim state, checkboxes, settings.
Resumable editing sessions — save an atlas and come back to it later.
Project assets have a unique icon (blue tile with an atlas grid + a gear) — easy to spot in the Content Browser. RMB → "Open Generated Atlas" jumps to the atlas produced by that session.
Automatic. On every Create Atlas the plugin writes a `_Project.uasset` next to the atlas:
To resume — double-click the `_Project.uasset` in the Content Browser. The editor opens with the state restored.
The success toast has an "Open Project" link — opens the project in a fresh window with clean memory. Handy after heavy atlas work.
Before v2.1 the only way to revisit a saved atlas was to re-import it. Slow on big atlases, and every manual tweak (moves, rotations) had to be redone.
With the Project asset — double-click and you're back exactly where you left off. State restores byte-for-byte.
Hit Save (`Ctrl+S`) in the editor toolbar — the session is saved without running Create Atlas. Handy for trying out alternate layouts without losing the current one.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.getting-started.d: First steps with Sprite Optimizer — installation and your first atlas.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.getting-started.б: For simple cases — select 2+ textures, RMB → Quick Atlas. An atlas is created instantly with Project Settings defaults, no editor involved.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.getting-started.с: Select 2+ textures in the Content Browser.
Hit F5 (Analyze) — the algorithm lays textures out into the atlas.
Optionally drag items by hand, change page size.
Hit Ctrl+Shift+Enter (Create Atlas) — atlas, sprites, and Material Instances are saved next to the sources.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.getting-started.у: The plugin ships via FAB Marketplace — after purchase it's available through Epic Games Launcher.
Open Epic Games Launcher → Library → Vault.
Find Sprite Optimizer and hit Install to Engine (or Add to project).
In Edit → Plugins, confirm Sprite Optimizer is enabled.
Supports UE 4.27 and 5.0–5.7. One plugin — all versions.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.getting-started.ч: Sprite Optimizer Project — saving and resuming sessions
plugin.spriteOptimizer.getting-started.ш: Open Content Browser, select one or more textures/sprites.
RMB → in the Sprite Optimizer section, pick an action:

| Action | Description | |----------|----------| | Optimize | Trim transparent edges | | Atlas | Open the full atlas editor | | Quick Atlas | Create atlas instantly with default settings | | Import Atlas | Reverse-engineer an existing atlas |
With multiple textures selected: Optimize, Atlas, Quick Atlas, Analyze. With one — Import Atlas is also available.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.descrip: Hotkeys, project settings, and technical details for Sprite Optimizer.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.h.norma: Normal maps / HDR textures look empty
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.h.проек: Project opens slowly on big atlases
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.h.стары: Legacy material errors on UE 4.27 ("partially loaded")
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.normal-: Fixed in v2.1. Old versions only supported 8-bit sources. Upgrade to v2.1.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.preview: Edit → Project Settings → Plugins → Sprite Optimizer → Editor Performance → Preview Quality

| Setting | Use case | |-----------|-----------| | Low (512 px) | Many 4K+ sources, limited RAM | | Medium (1024 px) | Default. Recommended for most projects | | High (2048 px) | Sharp previews at heavy zoom | | Full | No cap — full source resolution |
Affects editor preview only. The final atlas always exports at full resolution.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.project: Edit → Project Settings → Plugins → Sprite Optimizer

| Category | Settings | |-----------|-----------| | Optimization | Default Pixels Per Unit, Padding, Alpha Threshold | | Auto Selection | Min Savings Threshold for auto-pick | | Quick Atlas | Algorithm, Max Size, Size Mode, Padding, Optimize First, Allow Rotation, Auto Multi-Pack, Pad to x4, Preserve Position, Suffix | | Material | Default Sprite Material, Texture Parameter Name, Custom Parent Materials, Material Domain | | Output | Subfolder names, Suffix for optimized files | | Workflow | Verbose logs, notifications, Auto Refresh Content Browser |
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.slice-t: Fixed in v2.1. The checker background now renders only in the visible area.

plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.диалог-: | Input | Action | |------|----------| | `LMB` | Select region | | `LMB drag` (empty) | Draw new region | | `Del` | Delete selected region | | `R` | Mark as rotated | | `Mouse wheel` | Zoom | | `RMB / MMB drag` | Pan |
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.навигац: | Input | Action | |------|----------| | `Mouse wheel` | Zoom | | `MMB drag` | Pan | | `RMB drag` | Pan (alt) | | `Home` | Fit to atlas |
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.не-хват: Fixed in v2.1. Toolbar is included in the default layout.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.операци: | Shortcut | Action | |---------|----------| | `R` | Rotate selection 90° clockwise | | `Del` | Remove from atlas → Unplaced |
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.переклю: | Shortcut | Action | |---------|----------| | `G` | Grid | | `S` | Grid snap | | `F` | Free Move (no collisions) | | `P` | Rulers |

plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.редакти: | Input | Action | |------|----------| | `LMB` | Select item | | `Ctrl+LMB` | Add/remove from selection | | `LMB drag` (empty) | Rubber-band select | | `Ctrl+LMB drag` | Additive rubber band (XOR) | | `LMB drag` (on item) | Move | | `Shift+drag` | Axis lock — horizontal or vertical | | `Shift+Resize` | Proportional atlas-page resize | | `Ctrl+drag` | Force grid snap |
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.редакто: Set Preview Quality → Low (512 px cap). Final atlas quality isn't affected.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.старые-: Fixed in v2.1. The plugin detects stale cached materials and auto-rebuilds them.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.reference.техниче: | Parameter | Value | |----------|----------| | Supported engines | UE 4.27, 5.0–5.7 | | Type | Editor-only plugin | | Dependencies | Paper2D (stock engine plugin) | | Platforms | Windows, Mac, Linux | | Max atlas size | Up to 8192×8192 (configurable) | | Compression | BC7 with alpha for every exported texture | | Runtime cost | Zero — never ships in the packaged game | | Source files | Never mutated — originals always preserved | | Undo/Redo | 50 levels |
plugin.spriteOptimizer.tagline: Atlas packing, alpha trimming, auto-generated Material Instances, and reverse-engineering existing atlases — one editor for the entire 2D pipeline in Unreal.
plugin.spriteOptimizer.title: Sprite Optimizer
plugin.spriteOptimizer.video.title: Sprite Optimizer v2.1 — Paper2D Atlas Plugin
plugin.spriteOptimizer.полный-список-фун: Auto trim — strips transparent edges, tunable alpha threshold
Memory warnings — RAM estimate before heavy ops
50 levels of undo/redo — experiment without fear
Zero runtime cost — editor-only plugin, never ships in the packaged game
Pivot preservation — trimmed sprites stay visually in place in the scene
4 packing algorithms — Max Rects, Bin Pack, Fixed Grid, Tile
Import Atlas — reverse-engineer an existing atlas back into individual items
Quick Atlas — one-click atlas with default settings
Material Instances — auto-generated with pixel-based UV params, works in 3D and UMG
Multi-page atlases — auto-spawn extra pages when space runs out
Normal maps & HDR — 16-bit sources and HDR textures pack correctly
BC7 compression — high-quality compression with alpha support

site.about.body.p1: CRAFTCODE — I build and sell plugins and templates for Unreal Engine.
site.about.body.p2: CRAFT and CODE are a pair of assistant bots. They build everything you see here, argue about shape, measure each other, occasionally launch themselves at the ceiling.
